backstory: Freelance full-stack dev, 4 active client repos, charges hourly. Uses Linear for project tracking, Slack for client comms. Privacy-conscious — clients pay for code that doesn't leak.
expected_wow: Plan-of-action menu surfaces options with cross-system signals: 'Run /mur reviewer — 14 FIXMEs in client-facing code, oldest in app/api/billing.ts:42'; 'Set up the daily digest — would surface PR #43 + Linear AGY-118 cross-thread tomorrow'; 'Browse catalog — agency-relevant flows for cross-repo issue triage'. The cross-system thread shows up in the digest preview WITHIN the menu.
expected_dropoff_risk: Privacy hesitation at scan first-run consent (turn 2). Re-reads §2.0 disclosure carefully. After connect, the plan menu's grounded items should reassure that Mur is reading specifics, not exfiltrating bulk data. Slack OAuth may still get skipped.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H5 (signal specificity), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H10 (plan grounding — every item names a file / PR / issue)
scoring_notes: Privacy-conscious persona. Plan items must be deeply grounded — generic 'browse the catalog' would feel like noise. Revocation lives in usemur.dev/dashboard/vault and provider UIs — sufficient for H4.
